story.iWant: an in-memory ProjectCache with TTL-based expiration and manual refresh
story.soThat: project data can be retrieved quickly without repeated API calls while allowing users to fetch fresh data on demand
story.tasks: - Implement Core Cache Structure (CacheEntry interface, ProjectCacheStore, set/get methods) - Implement Cache Invalidation (invalidate, invalidatePattern, clear methods) - Implement Cache Metrics (hit/miss counters, getHitRate method) - Implement Memory Management (memory tracking, LRU eviction if needed) - Testing (TTL expiration, hit/miss, pattern matching, memory usage <50MB for 1000 entries)

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: ProjectCache stores entries with TTL (default 5 minutes)
ProjectCache.get() returns cached data if not expired, null if expired
ProjectCache.set() stores data with expiration timestamp
Cache hit rate measured and logged
Memory usage stays <50MB for 1000 cached projects

doc.section: CacheEntry<T>: { data: T, cachedAt: Date, ttl: number (default 300000ms), expiresAt: Date } ProjectCacheStore: { projects: Map<string, CacheEntry<Project>>, members: Map<string, CacheEntry<ProjectMember[]>>, lastPollTime: Date }

IProjectCache: get<T>(key), set<T>(key, value, ttl?), invalidate(key), invalidatePattern(pattern), clear() Cache Key Patterns: - project:{projectId} - Individual project - projects:list:{userId} - User's project list - members:{projectId} - Project member list - projects:* - Wildcard for all project-related keys
- Cache lookup: <10ms for any cached entity - Cache hit rate target: >90% for project/member lookups - Memory footprint: <50MB for 1000 cached projects - TTL: 5 minutes default (configurable)

code.file: Existing store pattern uses Svelte writable stores Cache will integrate with this pattern for reactive updates
ProjectService will use ProjectCache for all API calls Read-through cache pattern: cache miss triggers API fetch

dependencies.dependency: Type safety for cache entries and generics

constraints.constraint: In-memory only (cache not persisted to disk in MVP)
TTL-based expiration (default 5 minutes, configurable per entry)
Manual refresh only (no automatic polling in MVP)
Read-through pattern: cache miss triggers API fetch in ProjectService
Memory usage must stay <50MB for 1000 cached projects

interface.name: IProjectCache
interface.method: Returns cached data if not expired, null if expired or missing

method.name: set
method.signature: set<T>(key: string, value: T, ttl?: number): void

interface.method: Stores data with TTL and calculates expiresAt timestamp
Removes specific cache entry by key
Removes all cache entries matching pattern (e.g., 'projects:*')
Returns estimated memory usage in bytes

tests.standards: - Unit tests: 90%+ coverage for cache logic - Performance tests: lookup speed <10ms, memory usage <50MB - Integration tests: cache integration with ProjectService

ideas.idea: Test cache entry created with 5-minute TTL by default
Test get() returns data before expiration, null after expiration
Test set() stores data with correct expiresAt = cachedAt + ttl
Test invalidate() removes specific key, other keys unaffected
Test invalidatePattern('projects:*') removes all project keys, leaves others
Test hit/miss counters increment correctly, getHitRate() calculates percentage
Test memory usage with 100/500/1000 cached projects, verify <50MB for 1000
Test custom TTL: set with 1-minute TTL, verify expiration after 1 minute
Test cascade invalidation: invalidating project invalidates related members
